known_limitations_disclosed: Timeout test asserts codes.DeadlineExceeded specifically (via context.WithTimeout wrapping); a bare context cancellation from an upstream caller would surface as codes.Canceled instead -- both are non-nil, non-hanging errors satisfying the acceptance criteria, but only DeadlineExceeded is exercised by the test suite added here.

SplitProposer's interface (proposer.go) takes no context.Context, so GRPCSplitProposer's only per-call deadline knob is the constructor-configured timeout field; this is a pre-existing interface constraint, not something this subtask could change without touching proposer.go (out of scope).
